header_summary:
Greg Sanders, Steven Roose, and other contributors have advanced Bitcoin's scripting capabilities, integrating new primitives into Miniscript descriptors and proposing enhancements to Partially Signed Bitcoin Transactions (PSBTs) to support these advancements. The proposed modifications aim to improve transaction flexibility and safety by addressing template hash checks, rebindable signatures, and the introduction of new script fragments for nuanced signature handling. This effort underscores the ongoing evolution of Bitcoin's transaction and scripting infrastructure, inviting community feedback to refine the proposed solutions [link](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/9iwKoVRTokGsIwgHzGWO9ptfxAaIpJ_d8JY-uagbNnlKZK0WdUsl53IHO3kG2zcMmPObgDkHDltcVfZg7OAiZ5f11Tbt9e1vDW7GsOy-LeA=@protonmail.com/T/#u#mff9d9f1d7058940a9feb3c27de4d7b9dc93cbf86).

In parallel, significant progress has been made on the secp256k1 issue, with a consensus on the K_max protocol change leading to a new Pull Request in the Bitcoin Improvement Proposals repository. This development highlights the collaborative effort to refine Bitcoin's underlying protocols, illustrating the community's dedication to open discourse and iterative improvement [link](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/2106).

The need for a quantum-resistant upgrade to Bitcoin is being critically examined, considering the potential threat posed by quantum computing to its cryptographic foundations. Proposed strategies include integrating post-quantum cryptographic schemes and a phased migration framework to gradually introduce post-quantum support. This comprehensive analysis of technical, cryptographic, and economic considerations emphasizes the importance of proactive planning for Bitcoin's long-term security [link](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/a-discussion-on-quantum-resistant-upgrade-paths-for-bitcoin/2275).

Lastly, discussions on enhancing Segwit security and functionality through a new witness version and the use of Congestion Controlled Transactions (CCTV) underscore innovative approaches to improving transaction efficiency and security. These proposals, aiming for a soft fork implementation, reflect the continuous endeavor to advance blockchain technology's robustness and efficiency [link](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/a-quantum-resistance-script-only-using-op-ctv-op-txhash-and-no-new-signatures/2168/7).

recent_posts.title: Extensions to standard tooling for TEMPLATEHASH-CSFS-IK support
recent_posts.summary:
- Last year marked a milestone in expanding Bitcoin scripting capabilities.
- Innovations include the 'pk_i()' fragment for direct Taproot key use in scripts.
- The discussion continues on refining scripting and PSBT enhancements for Bitcoin.

recent_posts.title: BIP-352: Limiting the number of per-group recipients (K_max)
recent_posts.summary:
- Significant progress has been made on the secp256k1 issue, reaching consensus on K_max protocol change.
- A new Pull Request in the Bitcoin Improvement Proposals repository aims to enhance Bitcoin protocols.
- The Bitcoin community is urged to contribute insights, fostering collaborative and iterative improvement.

recent_posts.title: A Discussion on Quantum-Resistant Upgrade Paths for Bitcoin
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin's security may be compromised by quantum computing, necessitating a preemptive upgrade.
- Proposed solutions include a hybrid signature model and a new quantum-resistant address system.
- A phased migration strategy is recommended to gradually integrate post-quantum cryptographic schemes.

recent_posts.title: A quantum resistance script only using op_ctv/op_txhash and no new signatures
recent_posts.summary:
- A new witness version is proposed to replace key-path spending with script evaluation.
- The update aims to prevent signature forgery by eliminating fallback paths using NUMS/keys via a soft fork.
- An alternative involves CCTV for transactions, enhancing security, fee issues, and value flexibility.

recent_posts.title: Stateless VTXO Verification: Decoupling Custody from Implementation-Specific Stacks
recent_posts.summary:
- The email highlights the importance of external feedback for improving vtxo exits.
- It shows an openness to constructive criticism and a commitment to programming excellence.
- The sender values detailed reviews for enhancing functionality and reliability.

active_posts.title: Algorithm Agility for Bitcoin to maintain security in the face of quantum and classic breaks in the signature algorithms
active_posts.summary:
- Enhancing Bitcoin's security aims to address future quantum and classical algorithm threats.
- A dual signature system, with a primary and emergency backup algorithm, is proposed for resilience.
- The initiative ensures Bitcoin's reliability as a long-term store of value by preparing for advancements in cryptography.

active_posts.title: The limitations of cryptographic agility in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Cryptographic agility in Bitcoin explores user-selectable primitives for enhanced security.
- A new scheme, FancySig, introduces debate over adoption for post-quantum security versus risks.
- Consensus on upgrades and the impact of disabling EC operations are central challenges.

active_posts.title: Hourglass V2 Update
active_posts.summary:
- The Hourglass proposal now limits Bitcoin spending from certain outputs to one per block.
- Revised details and technical rationale are accessible via a provided GitHub link.
- Community feedback is encouraged to refine the proposal and enhance Bitcoin's security.

active_posts.title: Disposing of "dust attack" UTXOs
active_posts.summary:
- Dust attacks challenge on-chain wallets, prompting strategies for linking addresses via minor transactions.
- Proposed solutions include spending dust UTXOs on transaction fees, with considerations for address types.
- Implementation challenges involve user privacy risks, transaction rebroadcasting, and multisig complications.

active_posts.title: The future of the Bitcoin Core GUI
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core GUI faces deprecation debate due to maintenance and utility concerns.
- Proposed IPC interface could allow third-party GUIs, enhancing Bitcoin Core's modularity.
- Removing the GUI might hinder non-technical users and increase malware risks.

active_posts.title: Stealth addresses using nostr
active_posts.summary:
- Nostr uses Bitcoin's cryptographic techniques for secure key generation and payment reception.
- It improves on BIP 47 by allowing encrypted notifications and better user experience.
- The provided Python code helps developers integrate stealth payment capabilities for enhanced privacy.

today_in_history_posts.title: Multi CSV Transaction
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Praveen inquires about OP_CHECKSEQUENCEVERIFY's role in transactions.
- He questions if multiple CSV endpoints enable varied conditions after different delays.
- A figure illustrating his query is shared, seeking specific clarification on CSV's utility.

today_in_history_posts.title: Pizza for (lightning) bitcoins?
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A man used the lightning network for a real trade to buy pizza through a friend in London.
- The friend acted as a middleman, using a local shop to deliver the pizzas paid in Bitcoin.
- The man confirmed payment using a bolt11 invoice and a unique preimage code, 7241-a8c1, to receive his pizza.

today_in_history_posts.title: Workgroup lifecycle
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A cluster mempool working group was established on November 1, 2023, to manage technical discussions.
- By November 30, 2023, topics became publicly readable to share insights while keeping posting restricted.
- The group concluded as its project advanced to Bitcoin Core 28.0, integrating discussions into the main category.
